title: Remove unused getLogoPath and getPreviewDate functions
description:
The functions `getLogoPath()` and `getPreviewDate()` in src/utils/logoUtils.ts are defined but never used anywhere in the codebase.

## Details
- `getLogoPath()` is unused - SeasonalLogo component builds its own logo path inline
- `getPreviewDate()` is unused
- `parseISO` from date-fns is imported solely for `getPreviewDate()`

## Proposed Solution
Remove both functions and the `parseISO` import to eliminate dead code and reduce bundle size.

title: Consolidate duplicate CSS definitions across stylesheets
description:
Multiple CSS rules are duplicated across files, causing maintenance burden and potential specificity conflicts.

## Duplicates Found
- `.page` styles: index.css (lines 140-144), layout.css (lines 31-36)
- `.page-title` styles: index.css (lines 146-149), layout.css (lines 38-43)
- `.submit-button` styles: index.css (lines 161-173), contact.css (lines 102-124)
- `body` styles: index.css (lines 8-14), base.css (lines 20-27)
- `html, body, #root` height/width: index.css (lines 16-22), layout.css (lines 1-9)
- `.main-content` styles: index.css (lines 102-109), layout.css (lines 23-28)
- `.app-container` styles: index.css (lines 28-32), layout.css (lines 12-20)
- `.logo` styles: index.css (lines 126-138) vs App.css (lines 8-19) - conflicting definitions

## Proposed Solution
Consolidate duplicate rules into single authoritative locations. Remove duplicates from index.css where they already exist in dedicated style files.

title: Remove unused App.css file
description:
src/App.css contains Vite boilerplate CSS (logo spinning animation, .card, .read-the-docs) that appears unused by the application. The .logo class defined here conflicts with the .logo class in index.css.

## Proposed Solution
Remove App.css entirely if none of its styles are used. If the .logo hover effect is desired, consolidate it into index.css.

title: TypeScript Migration
Set up TypeScript configuration (tsconfig.json, dependencies)
Update Jest config for TypeScript and convert tests to .tsx
Convert src/utils/logoUtils.js to TypeScript with proper date types
Convert src/components/SeasonalLogo.jsx to TSX with props interface
Convert src/components/Header.jsx to TSX with props interface
Convert src/components/Footer.jsx to TSX
Convert src/pages/Home.jsx to TSX (includes AnimatedBird component)
Convert src/pages/Contact.jsx to TSX with form types
Convert src/pages/About.jsx and Crops.jsx to TSX
Convert src/App.jsx and main.jsx to TSX
Update README.md with current project info
description: README.md has had 0 updates while the project has had 95 commits. Update with current tech stack, setup instructions, deployment info, and project structure.

title: Add Helmet SEO metadata to Home and Contact pages
description:
The About and Crops pages include <Helmet> for SEO metadata, but Home and Contact do not.

## Proposed Solution
Add consistent <Helmet> tags to Home.tsx and Contact.tsx pages for SEO consistency across the site.

title: E3: Code Quality & Dead Code Cleanup
description: Epic for code quality improvements including unused CSS, dead code, missing 404 page, and component improvements.

title: Remove unused CSS classes and animation
description:
Several CSS classes in the stylesheets are never used by any component:

In src/styles/home.css:
- .home-title (line 6-10) - not used in Home.tsx
- .home-subtitle (line 12-17) - not used in Home.tsx
- .hero-text (line 57-65) - not used in Home.tsx
- @keyframes fadeInOut (line 145-150) - not referenced by any class

In src/styles/layout.css:
- .text-center (line 46-48) - utility class never used
- .mt-2 (line 50-52) - utility class never used
- .mt-4 (line 54-56) - utility class never used

In src/styles/contact.css:
- .page.contact-page (line 21-26) - Contact.tsx uses just 'page' class, not 'contact-page'

Action: Remove these dead CSS rules to reduce stylesheet size and improve maintainability.

title: Add 404 Not Found page with catch-all route
description:
The app has no 404 page. Navigating to any undefined route (e.g., /foo) shows a blank page with just the header and footer. React Router should have a catch-all route.

Fix in src/App.tsx:
1. Create src/pages/NotFound.tsx with a friendly 404 message and a link back to Home
2. Add a catch-all Route: <Route path='*' element={<NotFound />} />
3. Add a test in src/__tests__/NotFound.test.tsx

This is also important for SEO - search engine crawlers encountering dead links will see blank content instead of a proper 404 indicator.

title: Move react and react-dom from devDependencies to dependencies
description:
In package.json, react (^18.3.1) and react-dom (^18.3.1) are listed under devDependencies instead of dependencies. While Vite bundles everything at build time so this doesn't break the build, it's semantically incorrect - react and react-dom are runtime dependencies that ship to production.

This can confuse tools that analyze dependencies (e.g., npm audit, Dependabot, Snyk) and other developers reading the package.json.

File: package.json, lines 39-40. Move to the dependencies section.

title: Remove duplicate test (NavBar.test.tsx duplicates App.test.tsx)
description:
src/__tests__/NavBar.test.tsx and src/__tests__/App.test.tsx contain identical test logic - both render <App /> and check for the same 4 navigation links (Home, About, Crops, Contact). The NavBar.test.tsx is redundant.

NavBar.test.tsx: renders App and checks navigation links exist
App.test.tsx: renders App and checks navigation links exist (same checks)

Action: Delete src/__tests__/NavBar.test.tsx since App.test.tsx already covers the same assertions.

title: Remove stale Vite CSS preprocessor config
description:
In vite.config.ts (lines 13-17), there's a css.preprocessorOptions.css.additionalData config that attempts to inject an @import for index.css into CSS preprocessing. This configuration has no effect because Vite's CSS preprocessor options only apply to actual CSS preprocessors (Sass, Less, Stylus), not plain CSS files.

The index.css is already imported in main.tsx (line 3: import './index.css'), so this config is dead code.

File: vite.config.ts, remove lines 12-18 (the entire css block).

title: Rename project from 'vite-project' to 'coatesvillefarm'
description:
The package.json name field (line 2) is still the default Vite scaffold name 'vite-project'. This should be renamed to 'coatesvillefarm' or 'coatesvillefarm.com' to match the actual project.

File: package.json, line 2

title: Redirect www.coatesvillefarm.com to coatesvillefarm.com
description: Add a CloudFront Function to 301 redirect www requests to non-www canonical domain. Keeps everything in one distribution.

title: Canonicalize non-www domain: update sitemap and robots.txt
description: Sitemap and robots.txt reference www.coatesvillefarm.com but the canonical domain should be coatesvillefarm.com (non-www). Update all references to use non-www.

title: CSS cleanup: remove commented code and redundant font-family declarations
description:
Minor CSS cleanup items:

## 1. Commented-out CSS code
src/index.css (lines 191-199) has a commented-out `.navbar-logo` rule that appears unused.

## 2. Redundant font-family declarations
`font-family: var(--font-body)` is declared in base.css on the body element, which should cascade to all elements. Yet it is repeatedly redeclared on .app-container, .footer, .contact-form, .helper-text, .submit-button, .chirp-bubble, and .home-subtitle (10 occurrences across 5 files).

## Proposed Solution
- Remove commented-out code
- Remove redundant font-family declarations that inherit from body

title: Prevent double-fire on mobile touch events (onTouchStart + onClick)
description:
In src/pages/Home.tsx, the goose images and animated bird have both onClick and onTouchStart handlers that fire the same action (lines 79-81 and 144-146). On mobile devices, a tap fires both onTouchStart AND onClick, causing the audio to play twice (or restart immediately).

Fix options:
1. Remove onTouchStart handlers and rely on onClick alone (modern mobile browsers handle click events well with minimal delay)
2. If onTouchStart is needed for instant response, call e.preventDefault() in onTouchStart to suppress the subsequent onClick, or use a ref-based debounce

Files: src/pages/Home.tsx lines 79-81 (goose images) and lines 144-146 (animated bird)

title: E1: Image Optimization
description: Epic for image-related improvements. The site has 38MB of PNG images in public/assets/img/, many of which are 1-2MB each. This epic covers converting to modern formats, removing unused images, and implementing lazy loading.
close_reason: All children completed - PRs #65, #67 (unused images kept per user request)

title: Remove 11 unused image assets (17MB)
description:
11 image files in public/assets/img/ are never referenced in source code (neither directly nor via dynamic path construction). These add ~17MB to the deployed S3 bucket unnecessarily.

Unused images:
- flying-bird-1.png, flying-bird-2.png, flying-bird-3.png, flying-bird-4.png (replaced by northern-cardinal-*.png)
- flying-birds-all.png
- hero-birds.png
- logo-night.png (superseded by season-specific night logos)
- logo-summer-01.png (duplicate; logo-summer.png is used)
- logo.png (superseded by seasonal logos)
- swan-1.png, swan-2.png

Action: Delete these files and verify the site still renders correctly on all pages.
close_reason: Won't fix - images may be used in future

title: Convert PNG images to WebP with PNG fallback
description: All images are served as PNG, totaling 38MB. Converting to WebP would reduce size by 50-80% (est. 10-15MB total). The hero image alone is 1.9MB as PNG but likely ~400KB as WebP. Approach: 1. Use a build-time tool (e.g., sharp, imagemin, or squoosh-cli) to generate .webp versions alongside .png files 2. Use elements with and fallback for browser compatibility 3. Priority: hero.png (1.9MB), silo-rainbow.png (2.0MB), cloud-*.png (1.3-2.1MB each), logo-*.png (1.1-1.6MB each) Files to update: src/pages/Home.tsx, src/pages/About.tsx, src/components/SeasonalLogo.tsx, src/constants/index.ts (BIRD_FRAMES)

title: Add lazy loading for below-fold images
description:
Images below the fold (silo-rainbow.png on About page, goose/bird images on Home) should use loading='lazy' to improve initial page load. The hero image should NOT be lazy-loaded (it's above the fold).

Files to update:
- src/pages/About.tsx: Add loading='lazy' to the silo-rainbow img
- src/pages/Home.tsx: Add loading='lazy' to goose images (they're at the bottom of the hero)
- Consider adding fetchpriority='high' to the hero image for LCP optimization

title: Fix client-side routing: add server-side fallback for direct URL access
description: Subpages (/about, /crops, /contact) return 404 when accessed directly (e.g., shared links, search engine crawlers, browser refresh). The SPA client-side router only works when navigating from the homepage. Need a server-side catch-all that serves index.html for all routes so the client router can handle them. Options: configure hosting provider (CloudFront, S3, Netlify, etc.) to redirect all 404s to index.html, or switch to SSR/SSG for better SEO.

title: Accessibility Improvements
Add htmlFor/id attributes to Contact form labels and inputs
Add aria-describedby for form error messages and helper text
Add keyboard support to interactive geese (role=button, tabIndex, onKeyDown)
Add keyboard support to AnimatedBird component
Add aria-hidden=true to decorative emojis in Footer
Add aria-current=page to active navigation links
Add focus trap to mobile menu when open
Add visible focus indicators for all interactive elements
Add structured data (JSON-LD) for local business SEO
description:
The site has good meta tags and a sitemap but no structured data (Schema.org JSON-LD). Adding a LocalBusiness schema would help search engines understand the farm's location, contact info, and type of business.

Add a <script type='application/ld+json'> block to index.html or inject via react-helmet-async in App.tsx with:
- @type: LocalBusiness (or Farm subtype)
- name: Coatesville Farm
- address: 14072 Old Ridge Road, Beaverdam, VA
- telephone: (804) 449-6016
- email: coatesvillefarm@gmail.com
- url: https://coatesvillefarm.com

Also consider adding an OpenGraph image meta tag for social media sharing (og:image), and ensuring canonical URLs are set on each page.

Create src/constants/index.ts with ANIMATION_TIMING, FORM_CONFIG, BIRD_FRAMES
Create src/utils/audioUtils.ts with playAudio helper function
Remove duplicate season/night logic from SeasonalLogo.jsx - use logoUtils instead
Replace hardcoded colors in index.css and contact.css with CSS variables
Consolidate duplicate cloud animation CSS - keep only in home.css
Move navbar styles from index.css to dedicated navbar.css
Remove unused useState/useEffect imports from App.jsx
Remove console.log from Contact.jsx and replace alert() with toast notification
E4: Accessibility Improvements
description: Epic for accessibility (a11y) improvements including skip navigation, ARIA landmarks, and screen reader enhancements.

title: Add skip-to-content navigation link
description:
There is no skip navigation link. Keyboard users must tab through all nav links before reaching page content. This is a WCAG 2.1 Level A requirement (2.4.1 Bypass Blocks).

Fix:
1. Add a visually hidden 'Skip to main content' link as the first focusable element in the app
2. It should become visible on focus (CSS: .skip-link:focus { position: static; })
3. Link target: the <main> element in App.tsx (add id='main-content' to <main>)
4. The .visually-hidden class already exists in contact.css - move it to base.css for reuse

File: src/App.tsx (add skip link before Header), src/styles/base.css (add skip-link styles)

title: Add ARIA landmark role to nav element
description:
The Header component (src/components/Header.tsx) uses a <nav> element which implicitly has role='navigation', but there is no aria-label to distinguish it from other potential nav landmarks. While there's currently only one nav, best practice is to label it for screen reader users.

Also, the mobile menu overlay doesn't indicate its expanded/collapsed state to screen readers beyond aria-expanded on the button. Consider adding aria-controls pointing to the nav links container.

Files: src/components/Header.tsx
- Add aria-label='Main navigation' to the <nav> element
- Add id='main-nav' to the nav links div
- Add aria-controls='main-nav' to the menu toggle button

title: Add screen reader text for footer contact info icons
description: The Footer component (src/components/Footer.tsx) uses emoji icons (pin, phone, envelope) with aria-hidden='true', which is correct. However, there is no screen-reader-only text to convey what the icon represents. A screen reader user hears just the link text without context like 'Address:', 'Phone:', 'Email:'. Fix: Add visually hidden elements before each link: - Address: before the map link - Phone: before the phone link - Email: before the email link File: src/components/Footer.tsx

title: Refactor Header navigation links to use data mapping
description:
The navigation links in Header.tsx (lines 46-77) repeat the same pattern four times with only the path and label changing.

## Current State
Each link has identical onClick, className, and aria-current logic repeated.

## Proposed Solution
Extract navigation items into a constant array and map over it:
```tsx
const NAV_ITEMS = [
  { path: '/', label: 'Home' },
  { path: '/about', label: 'About' },
  { path: '/crops', label: 'Crops' },
  { path: '/contact', label: 'Contact' },
];
```
Then render via .map(). This reduces ~30 lines to ~10 lines and makes adding/removing nav items trivial.

title: Refactor Home component goose images to reduce duplication
description:
Three goose images in Home.tsx (lines 50-79) share nearly identical structure with only minor differences (image source, className modifier, alt text, click handler).

## Proposed Solution
Define goose data as an array and map over it, or extract a GooseImage component that accepts props for the variations. This reduces code duplication and improves maintainability.

title: Add audio preload='none' for non-critical audio files
description:
In src/pages/Home.tsx, three audio elements use preload='auto' (lines 85-86, 136), causing the browser to download all audio files immediately on page load even if the user never interacts with the geese or bird.

These audio files are non-critical - they only play on user interaction. Changing to preload='none' (or 'metadata') would reduce initial page load by avoiding 3 unnecessary audio downloads.

Files: src/pages/Home.tsx
- Line 85: <audio ref={gooseAudioRef} preload='auto'> -> preload='none'
- Line 86: <audio ref={goslingAudioRef} preload='auto'> -> preload='none'
- Line 136: <audio ref={chirpAudio} preload='auto'> -> preload='none'

title: Infrastructure & SEO Fixes
description: Epic for infrastructure and SEO improvements to coatesvillefarm.com

close_reason: Completed in PR #57 — reCAPTCHA v3 extracted to useRecaptcha hook

title: Fix undefined CSS variable --color-white
description:
src/styles/home.css (line 23) uses `var(--color-white)` but this variable is not defined in variables.css. Only `--color-surface: #ffffff` is defined.

## Proposed Solution
Either:
1. Replace `var(--color-white)` with `var(--color-surface)`, or
2. Add `--color-white: #ffffff` to variables.css

title: Add missing apple-touch-icon.png and proper favicon setup
description:
In index.html (line 14), the apple-touch-icon points to /favicon.ico, but iOS requires a 180x180 PNG file for the home screen icon. Using an .ico file results in a blurry or missing icon on iOS devices.

Fix:
1. Create proper apple-touch-icon.png (180x180) from the farm logo
2. Add various favicon sizes: favicon-16x16.png, favicon-32x32.png
3. Consider adding a site.webmanifest for Android PWA support with theme colors matching the farm green palette
4. Update index.html <link> tags accordingly

File: index.html, public/ directory

title: Add dist/ to .gitignore
description: The dist/ build output directory is not gitignored, causing 40+ large binary file warnings from stringer. Add dist/ to .gitignore and remove any tracked dist/ files.

title: Add .beads/ and .stringer/ to .gitignore
description: The .beads/ directory (local Dolt database) and .stringer/ directory generate false positive signals. Add both to .gitignore.

title: Standardize CSS variable naming (--color-bg vs --color-background)
description:
Both `--color-background` and `--color-bg` are defined in variables.css with the same value (#f1fce9). The codebase uses both interchangeably.

## Proposed Solution
Standardize on one variable name (preferably `--color-background` as it is more descriptive) and update all usages of `--color-bg`.

title: Upgrade vite from 6.3.5 to 7.x (3 CVEs)
description: vite 6.3.5 has 3 known vulnerabilities: CVE-2025-62522 (server.fs.deny bypass via backslash on Windows), CVE-2025-58751 (middleware may serve files with same name as public dir), CVE-2025-58752 (server.fs settings not applied to HTML files). Upgrade to vite 7.1.11 or latest.

title: Standardize component function declaration style
description:
The codebase mixes function declaration styles for components:

## Arrow functions used in:
- App.tsx: `const App = () => (...)`
- Footer.tsx: `const Footer = () => {...}`
- SeasonalLogo.tsx: `const SeasonalLogo = ({...}) => {...}`

## Function declarations used in:
- Header.tsx: `export default function Header() {...}`
- Home.tsx: `export default function Home() {...}`
- About.tsx, Crops.tsx, Contact.tsx

## Proposed Solution
Per project standards, standardize on function declarations for top-level components.

title: Add Lambda unit tests
description:
The Lambda function (lambda/src/index.ts, lambda/src/emails.ts) has zero test coverage. There's no test directory or test configuration in the lambda/ directory.

Tests needed:
1. handler: OPTIONS returns 200 with CORS headers
2. handler: Missing body returns 400
3. handler: Honeypot-filled submissions return 200 silently
4. handler: Rate limiting after 5 requests from same IP
5. handler: reCAPTCHA verification failure returns 400
6. handler: Successful submission sends both notification and confirmation emails
7. handler: Email validation rejects invalid addresses
8. handler: Field length validation
9. verifyRecaptcha: Skips when RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY is empty
10. verifyRecaptcha: Handles network errors gracefully
11. getCorsOrigin: Returns matching origin from ALLOWED_ORIGINS
12. getCorsOrigin: Returns default when origin not in allowed list

Setup: Add jest.config.js in lambda/, mock @aws-sdk/client-ses and fetch.

title: Add preload hints for critical hero image and fonts
description: The hero image (1.9MB) and Google Fonts are render-blocking or LCP-critical but have no preload hints. In index.html: 1. Add to start loading the hero image before React hydrates 2. Add (the fonts.googleapis.com link exists but fonts.gstatic.com preconnect is missing) 3. Consider adding fetchpriority='high' to the hero in Home.tsx This should improve Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) score.

title: Add missing tests for ErrorBoundary, useRecaptcha, and Header mobile menu
description:
Several components/hooks have no dedicated tests:

1. ErrorBoundary (src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx): No test for error state rendering. Should test that when a child throws, the fallback UI renders with 'Something went wrong' heading and refresh button. Also test the custom fallback prop.

2. useRecaptcha (src/hooks/useRecaptcha.ts): No tests at all. Should test:
   - Returns empty string when RECAPTCHA_SITE_KEY is not set
   - Loads the reCAPTCHA script when site key is set
   - Cleans up script on unmount
   - getToken returns empty string when not ready

3. Header mobile menu (src/components/Header.tsx): No test for mobile menu toggle, focus trap, or Escape key closing the menu. The focus trap is complex logic that should be tested.

4. Contact form submission: Only validation is tested, not the actual submit flow (fetch mock, success/error states, honeypot bypass, loading state).

5. Lambda handler: No tests for the Lambda function at all - no test directory or test files in lambda/. Should add tests for rate limiting, reCAPTCHA verification, honeypot, CORS, email sending.

title: E2: CI/CD & Security Hardening
description: Epic for CI/CD pipeline improvements and security fixes including outdated GitHub Actions, Node.js version pinning, and dependency vulnerabilities.

title: Upgrade GitHub Actions to latest versions (v3 -> v4)
description:
The deploy workflow (.github/workflows/deploy.yml) uses outdated action versions:
- actions/checkout@v3 -> @v4
- actions/setup-node@v3 -> @v4
- jakejarvis/s3-sync-action@master -> pin to a specific version/SHA for supply chain security

Using @v3 misses Node.js 20 runner support and security fixes. Pinning to exact SHAs is best practice for supply chain security.

Also: the build-and-test job uses 'npm install --legacy-peer-deps' while the deploy job uses 'npm ci'. These should both use 'npm ci' for reproducible builds.

title: Upgrade CI Node.js from 18 to 22 LTS
description:
The CI workflow (.github/workflows/deploy.yml) pins Node.js 18, which reaches EOL in April 2025. Node.js 22 LTS is current (active until April 2027).

Lines to change in .github/workflows/deploy.yml:
- Line 20: node-version: '18' -> '22'
- Line 46: node-version: '18' -> '22'

Also add an .nvmrc file with '22' to ensure consistent local development versions.

title: Fix CDK Lambda runtime mismatch (NODEJS_20_X vs nodejs24.x)
description:
The CDK stack (infrastructure/lib/contact-form-stack.ts line 92) specifies lambda.Runtime.NODEJS_20_X but the SAM template (lambda/template.yaml line 8) specifies nodejs24.x. These are out of sync.

Since the SAM template was already upgraded to nodejs24.x, the CDK stack should be updated to match: lambda.Runtime.NODEJS_22_X (Node.js 22 is the latest Lambda-supported LTS runtime as of early 2026, or NODEJS_24_X if available in the CDK version).

The CDK code comment 'Node.js 20.x is the latest LTS version supported by Lambda' (line 91) is also stale.

File: infrastructure/lib/contact-form-stack.ts, line 92

title: Restrict S3 bucket policy to CloudFront OAI/OAC
description:
The S3 bucket policy (iam/bucket-policy/s3-bucket-policy.json) grants public read access to all principals ('Principal': '*'). This means anyone can access the bucket directly, bypassing CloudFront.

Best practice is to restrict access to the CloudFront distribution only, using either:
- Origin Access Identity (OAI) - legacy but simple
- Origin Access Control (OAC) - modern, recommended by AWS

This prevents direct S3 access, ensures all traffic goes through CloudFront (for caching, HTTPS, WAF), and reduces the attack surface.

File: iam/bucket-policy/s3-bucket-policy.json
close_reason: Completed in PR #64 - documented as manual CloudFront follow-up

title: Add Content Security Policy and security headers
description:
The site has no Content Security Policy (CSP) or other security headers. Since it's served via CloudFront + S3, these should be added via CloudFront response headers policy or a CloudFront Function.

Recommended headers:
1. Content-Security-Policy: Restrict script/style sources to self, Google reCAPTCHA, and Google Fonts
2. X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
3. X-Frame-Options: DENY (prevent clickjacking)
4. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
5. Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()

This can be implemented as a CloudFront response headers policy in the CDK stack or as a manual CloudFront configuration.

title: Replace setInterval bird animation with requestAnimationFrame
description:
The AnimatedBird component in src/pages/Home.tsx (lines 107-119) uses two setInterval timers: one at 120ms for frame cycling and one at 16ms (~60fps) for position updates. The 16ms interval is effectively trying to match requestAnimationFrame but without the benefits of rAF:

1. setInterval at 16ms is unreliable (browser may throttle inactive tabs)
2. It doesn't sync with the display refresh rate
3. Two separate intervals cause unnecessary re-renders (each setFrameIndex and setPositionX triggers a render)

Fix:
- Replace the 16ms position interval with requestAnimationFrame
- Combine frame cycling and position into a single animation loop
- Use a ref for position instead of state to avoid re-renders per frame, and apply position via ref-based DOM manipulation
- Consider using CSS transforms/animations instead of JS for the position (the bird already has a CSS floatGlide animation that conflicts with the JS positioning)

File: src/pages/Home.tsx, AnimatedBird component (lines 102-159)

title: Lambda Node.js 24 Runtime Upgrade
description:
Upgrade the contact form Lambda function from Node.js 20 (EOL) to Node.js 24.

## Background
Node.js 20 has reached end-of-life and the Lambda runtime needs to be upgraded to Node.js 24. The console has already been updated; these issues track the code changes needed to align the infrastructure-as-code.

## Scope
- SAM template runtime specification
- TypeScript type definitions
- TypeScript compiler target
- Testing and validation

## Files Affected
- lambda/template.yaml
- lambda/package.json
- lambda/tsconfig.json
close_reason: Completed in PR #55 - all children closed

title: Update SAM template runtime to nodejs24.x
description:
Update the AWS SAM template to specify Node.js 24 runtime.

## File
`lambda/template.yaml` line 8

## Change
```yaml
# Before
Runtime: nodejs20.x

# After
Runtime: nodejs24.x
```

## Notes
- The console has already been updated to Node.js 24
- This change aligns the infrastructure-as-code with the deployed runtime

title: Update @types/node to v24
description:
Update the TypeScript type definitions for Node.js 24.

## File
`lambda/package.json` line 16

## Change
```json
// Before
"@types/node": "^20.0.0"

// After
"@types/node": "^24.0.0"
```

## Notes
- Ensures TypeScript type definitions match the runtime version
- Run `npm install` after updating to fetch new types

title: Update TypeScript target to ES2024
description:
Update the TypeScript compiler target to ES2024 to leverage newer JavaScript features available in Node.js 24.

## File
`lambda/tsconfig.json` line 3

## Change
```json
// Before
"target": "ES2020"

// After
"target": "ES2024"
```

## Optional Enhancement
Also update the `lib` option to match:
```json
"lib": ["ES2024"]
```

## Notes
- Node.js 24 supports ES2024 features natively
- This is optional but allows use of newer language features
- No breaking changes expected in existing code

title: Test Lambda after Node.js 24 upgrade
description:
Validate that the Lambda function works correctly after the Node.js 24 upgrade.

## Testing Checklist
- [ ] Build the Lambda with `npm run build`
- [ ] Deploy using `sam deploy` (or existing deployment method)
- [ ] Test contact form submission end-to-end
- [ ] Verify SES emails are sent correctly
- [ ] Check CloudWatch logs for any runtime errors or warnings
- [ ] Confirm reCAPTCHA verification still works

## Potential Issues to Watch
- Native `fetch` API (introduced in Node 18) should continue to work
- AWS SDK v3 compatibility with Node.js 24
- Any deprecated APIs that may have been removed

## Rollback
If issues are found, the console can be reverted to Node.js 20 while investigating
